steps.title: Keep subject and verb together
steps.content: The subject is “the Huntington Library in California,” and the main verb is “received.” The blank falls directly between that subject and verb.

steps.title: Avoid a subject break
steps.content: Choice A inserts a comma, Choice B inserts a colon, and Choice C inserts a dash. Each mark wrongly separates the subject from its verb. The sentence needs no punctuation after “California.” The answer is D.
